name: Minnesota Dietitian License
description: Already licensed as a dietitian? Here's whether your license transfers to Minnesota - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

provider.name: Board of Dietetics and Nutrition Practice

address.description: Minnesota Board of Dietetics and Nutrition Practice, 335 Randolph Avenue, Suite 170, St. Paul, MN 55102

renewalCycle: Annual (by Nov 30) + 45 CE hours per 3 years

requiredDocuments.name: Notarized CDR Registration Card
requiredDocuments.description: A notarized copy of your current, active CDR registration card or official exam results.

requiredDocuments.name: Official College Transcripts
requiredDocuments.description: Showing a master's or doctoral degree from an ACEND-accredited program.

requiredDocuments.name: Supervised Practice Documentation
requiredDocuments.description: Verification of at least 900 hours of supervised pre-professional practice.

requiredDocuments.name: Criminal Background Check
requiredDocuments.description: Completed background check ($32 fee paid as part of the application).

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does a Minnesota dietitian license cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: The total initial cost is $282: $100 application fee, $150 initial license fee, and $32 criminal background check fee. Annual renewal is $75, due by November 30 each year.

mainEntity.name: What are the continuing education requirements for Minnesota dietitians?

acceptedAnswer.text: Minnesota requires 45 continuing education units (CEUs) every three years for state license renewal. Additionally, CDR registration requires 75 CPEUs every five years — many activities satisfy both requirements.

mainEntity.name: Does Minnesota require a notarized CDR card?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Minnesota specifically requires a notarized copy of your current CDR registration card or official examination results as part of the license application. This is an unusual requirement among states.

mainEntity.name: Does Minnesota have reciprocity for dietitians?

acceptedAnswer.text: No interstate compact currently covers dietitian practice in Minnesota. To work in Minnesota, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to Minnesota?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 Weeks.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in Minnesota while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most applicants need to wait for full approval before starting work because the state does not clearly offer a temporary or provisional credential.
